section.title: Overview
section.para: A gazetteer maps place names to place records. The opennlp.tools.geo.Gazetteer interface is the one seam every lookup goes through: a bundled table, a downloaded dataset, an ingested database, and a remote service client are all implementations of this one interface, and any of them can be handed to the components that consume it. Each record is a GazetteerEntry carrying the canonical and alternate names, a point location, an optional bounding box, the ISO 3166-1 country code, a coarse feature class, the population, and dataset-specific extras as provenance-tagged attributes.

The opennlp-geo module ships three file-backed implementations: BundledGazetteer over the bundled Natural Earth populated-places table, GeoNamesGazetteer over a user-downloaded GeoNames extract, and OvertureGazetteer over an Overture divisions export. All implementations are immutable and thread-safe after loading, so one instance can be shared across threads.

section.title: User-supplied places
section.para: UserGazetteer loads places a public dataset does not know, for example customer sites or internal facility names, from a user-authored file, so they take part in geocoding and everything downstream of it. The file holds one place per line with tab-separated columns in this order, where trailing columns may be omitted and an empty column means absent: record id, canonical name, alternate names separated by |, latitude, longitude, country code, feature class, population, bounding box as west,south,east,north, containment chain separated by |, and one key=value attribute per further column. Lines starting with # and blank lines are skipped.

A row needs either both coordinates or a bounding box; with only a bounding box the point location becomes the box's center. Free-text metadata such as a postal address belongs in an attribute column: it is carried verbatim on the entry and is never parsed or matched. The following file, whose columns are separated by single tab characters, defines a plant with an address attribute and a campus located through its bounding box alone.
# id name alternates lat lon cc class pop bbox containment attributes plant-3 Acme Plant 3 Plant 3|The Plant 33.75 -84.39 US POI 0 Georgia|Fulton County address=123 Main St, Atlanta GA 30303 campus-east Acme Campus East US POI 0 -84.5,33.6,-84.2,33.9

section.title: Overlaying a base gazetteer
section.para: OverlayGazetteer composes a base gazetteer with user-supplied changes: additions from a second gazetteer, and suppression rules hiding base entries. Consumers see one merged view through the same Gazetteer interface, so every component downstream picks the changes up without knowing they exist. Additions rank first in lookups. Suppressions hide entries of the base only, never additions, so a rule plus an addition with the same name replaces a base entry.

A suppression rule is a place name with optional country and feature-class filters, one rule per line with tab-separated columns. The following file removes the city of Mobile, Alabama, from a corpus where that reading is always noise, while leaving every other Mobile-named record intact.
# name country feature class Mobile US CITY
